major_tropes: The Isolated Location With No Escape
The Threat That Cannot Be Reasoned With
The Rot Beneath the Surface of Something Normal
Survival Requires Abandoning Who You Were
plot_template: Characters in an isolated or trapped situation encounter a threat that defies their rational frameworks. As they attempt to survive, the threat forces them to confront something internal — a trauma, a relationship, a belief — that the horror is externalizing. Survival is never guaranteed and always costs something permanent.
character_archetypes: The Protagonist Whose Flaw the Horror Exploits
The Skeptic Who Dies Because They Wouldn't Believe
The Child or Innocent Who Sees Clearly
The Monster That Is Also a Mirror
world_building_notes: The setting should feel claustrophobic and specific. The rules of the threat must be internally consistent, even if they defy physics — inconsistent monsters are not scary, they're just confusing. Horror is built through dread (anticipation) not shock alone. Every scene should feel slightly wrong before anything visibly is.
emotional_core: Dread, the fragility of safety, the horror of helplessness, survival that leaves you changed
